title: Fanout Pub/Sub and Subscriber Responsibilities · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for fanout pub/sub and subscriber responsibilities and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use fanout routing when every binding should match and record the resulting copy count and storage responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Design exclusive server-named subscriptions for session-scoped state and account for disconnect, binding, declaration, and cleanup races; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility
steps.instruction:
Order the Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility RabbitMQ and Go messaging evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility, keep Subscriber Queue Per Responsibility connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7412 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Give each independent subscriber responsibility its own queue so every responsibility can receive and acknowledge its copy; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Late Subscriber Semantics
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Late Subscriber Semantics: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7462 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove one queue per subscriber responsibility and explicit late-subscriber behavior; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement owner; then exhaust retry age before attempts reach their maximum. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Late Subscriber Semantics.
steps.why: Retrieving Late Subscriber Semantics through the concrete a Go subscriber responsibility and queue lifecycle map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Late Subscriber Semantics, keep Late Subscriber Semantics connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: State whether late subscribers miss prior events, receive retained queue backlog, or require a stream or database replay path; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Late Subscriber Semantics, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A new fanout binding automatically receives messages published before it existed. In Go, multiple goroutines consuming one queue still implement competing consumers, not broadcast.
